_meta.doc: Species catalog. v0.2.0 introduces the four-species saproxylic core for the Białowieża beech-log scenario. Citations are short-form; full bibliography belongs in research/ (TBD).

evidence_level_ladder.locality_observed: Documented in a peer-reviewed paper from the specific scenario locality
evidence_level_ladder.regional_typical: Documented in the broader region's fauna list (e.g., European saproxylic for Białowieża)
evidence_level_ladder.genus_documented: Genus is well-documented in this guild/niche; species inferred
evidence_level_ladder.inferred_from_guild: No species-level documentation in this niche; presence inferred from guild ecology

_meta._audit_note_v0_2_0: All four species entered at 'regional_typical' — they appear in European saproxylic fauna lists (Speight 1989, Stokland et al. 2012) but I have not yet verified each against the published Białowieża inventory. Promote to 'locality_observed' after that audit.

trametes_versicolor.biology_notes: White-rot polypore. Establishes on partly-decayed sapwood with moisture > 0.4. Decomposes lignin + cellulose, releases fungal biomass (hyphae + spore-bearing pore tissue) that fuels fungivores. Mature bracket forms over ~30 days in optimal conditions; thalli persist for years.

ceratophysella_denticulata.biology_notes: Hypogastruridae. Consumes fungal hyphae and spores in moist-rotted wood. Asexual / parthenogenetic reproduction common. Eggs hatch in 7–10 days in temperate woodland; v0.3.0 uses 7 days as the egg duration.

glomeris_marginata.biology_notes: Glomeridae. Consumes partly-decayed wood + leaf litter; requires wood_decay_stage > 0.3 to access lignin breakdown products. Pill-rolling defense against most invertebrate predators but not centipedes the size of L. forficatus. Eggs ~30 days to hatch. Real adult-to-adult breeding cycle is 1+ year (Hopkin & Read 1992); v0.5.0 uses 70-day cooldown as a compromise that keeps populations dynamic over the 120-day scenario.

oniscus_asellus.biology_notes: Oniscidae. Larger and more cosmopolitan than Glomeris; tolerates drier substrates and broader decay-stage range (wood_decay_stage >= 0.2). Slower predator-escape than Glomeris but lacks the conglobation defense, so Lithobius takes them readily. Marsupial brood pouch; eggs incubate in the female until hatched.

neobisium_muscorum.biology_notes: Neobisiidae. Small soft-body predator with pincer-like pedipalps. Hunts springtails, mites, and small soft prey in moist litter and rotting wood crevices. Cannot tackle prey larger than ~6mm body length; doesn't compete directly with Lithobius which targets bigger prey. Has a venomous gland in the pedipalp tip.

lithobius_forficatus.biology_notes: Lithobiidae. Active predator hunting smaller arthropods in galleries and surface crevices. Venom mandibles. In v0.2.0 the predation success is deterministic when in-range; future versions add prey-flee + escape rolls. Eggs laid in soil cracks, ~40 days to hatch; sub-adult stadia elided.
